MonoBehaviour component fileID 123183794 (m_Script guid 50410efda0128f248aadbf8266a903cb) matches the provided TrafficLights script. Serialized refs: Red -> {fileID: 8569639267829841366}, Green -> {fileID: 2097357867}, Yellow -> {fileID: 888664352}. Let Start() coroutine run to cover full cycle: 0–30s Green on/Red off; at 30s log("on"), 30–35s Yellow on/Green off; at 35s log("on"), 35–65s Red on/Yellow off. Note: GameObject has no Rigidbody and no Collider/EventTrigger listed; this Trigger is used as a timed wait to observe automatic state changes. If a Collider/EventTrigger exists at runtime, use it to legally bind the wait; otherwise expose a public no-arg method to advance state for direct triggering.

MonoBehaviour_0 with m_Script guid f7de224e5fee443418417f9f2bfa2998 (type:3) corresponds to class Scoremanager attached to GameObject 'ScoreManager'. Scene config: winAmount=2; StarValue overridden to 1 in PrefabInstance; ScoreText/AmountUI/Timer are wired via Prefab modifications; winlosescript ref fileID=93502226. Scoremanager.changescore(int) triggers winlosescript.Winlevel() when score == winAmount, but changescore requires an int parameter and there is no Collider/EventTrigger on this object. To validate the win path despite lack of a public parameterless API, directly invoke WinLose.Winlevel() on the referenced component to simulate the condition of having collected enough stars.
